rxn_class_summary: Catalyst- and oxidant-free electrochemical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-arylacrylamides to 3-CF3-substituted oxindoles using TfNHNHBoc (CF3SO2NHNHBoc) as the CF3 source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of TfNHNHBoc (reagent 1) to a diazene intermediate I that fragments to release •CF3; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: diazene CF3SO2-N=N-Boc (I) from 2e- anodic oxidation of TfNHNHBoc
trifluoromethyl radical (•CF3) generated after loss of SO2, N2, tBu•
alkyl C-centered radical II from •CF3 addition to acrylamide alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical III from intramolecular ipso/ortho radical cyclization onto arene
cyclohexadienyl cation IV from anodic oxidation of III
trifluoromethylated oxindole product 3 after deprotonation of IV

electron_flow: TfNHNHBoc is oxidized at the graphite anode (2e-) to diazene I, which fragments (loss of SO2, N2, tBu•) to give •CF3; the radical adds to the acrylamide alkene to form C-radical II that undergoes intramolecular cyclization onto the aryl ring to give cyclohexadienyl radical III; III is further anodically oxidized to cation IV, which loses a proton to give oxindole 3, while H+ is reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: 3 equiv. BHT fully suppressed product formation, indicating a free-radical intermediate

notes: CV showed reagent 1 oxidizes at 1.45 V vs SCE, lower than substrate 2a (~2.03 V), supporting that 1 is oxidized first at the anode. Control with no electricity gave 0% yield. Gram-scale (7 mmol) reaction gave 3a in 55% yield. No oxindole product was observed without electricity.
